codeReview.summary: All three blocking issues from the original student-onboarding feature are properly fixed. (1) The onboarding_complete flag is now only set to true on the final step (isLastStep check in handleNext). (2) Gender and fitnessLevel are validated as required before allowing Step 5 completion. (3) DiagnosticQuiz now calls submitAssessment() API instead of calculating level locally.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill specifies using 'agent-browser' for UI validation after implementing components, but the worker did not perform interactive browser checks. Verification was limited to typecheck, lint, and Python syntax checks. While this is a deviation from the skill procedure, it may be justified given the pre-existing build failures (styled-jsx SSR issue) that would prevent the Next.js app from starting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true but no interactiveChecks were performed. The handoff shows only command-based verification.

summary: The foundation-fix-4-onboarding-logic feature successfully implements all three required fixes: (1) onboarding_complete now only set true after Step 5 via isLastStep flag, (2) gender and fitnessLevel validated as required fields with proper error messages, (3) diagnostic quiz now calls submitAssessment() API for server-side level calculation. Code changes are minimal and focused. No blocking issues found.
